The summer will be spent In Bev-

DISPATCHING BY PHONE WILL


THROW OPERATORS OUT OF WORK

pages.raw_text:
(. Plans for the elimination Of the
telegraph in train dispatching on
the Great Northern railway will be
tried out through this part Of the
territory within the next few
weeks, and 1t is not improbable that
as result OF the experiment, the
telephone will ultimately take the
place Of the key and sounder all
along Jim Hills road. Telephoning
of train dispatches will be taken
up on the Spokane division begin
ming this week, according to infor-
nation that has been secured from
local officials.


On the Kalispell division Of the
Great Northern, the dispatching of
trains by the use of the telephone
has already been tried out, 1t Is
declared, and, apparently, it Is q
success. With the plan now map
ped out in effect, a large item Of
expense will be cut out ana dan
gers Of accidents from operators
along the line being asleep on the
job will be lowered to a minimum.


On the Spokane division alone,
from Troy, Mont, to Leavenworth,
Wash. the telephone scheme will
displace three dispatchers, who are
drawing salary Of $14O per month
each. The system will be SO ar
